sections.paragraph:
fut. יִלְכֹּד.-

(1) to take, to catch animals in a net or in snares (prop. to strike with a net, compare Arab. لكد to strike), Amo 3:5 Psa 35:8 in a pit, Jer 18:22 take as captives, soldiers in war, Num 21:32 Jos 11:12 Jud. 8:12 to take, i.e. to capture a city, Jos 8:21 10:1 11:10 . Metaph. Job 5:13, “he takes (snares) the wise in their own craftiness;” Pro 5:22.

(2) to intercept, to take before (auffangen), Jud. 7:24 , וְלִכְדוּ לָהֶם אֶת־הַמַּיִם “take the water before them.”

(3) to take, to choose any one by lot. Compare אָחַז No. 7. Jos 7:14, הַשֵּׁבֶט אֲשֶׁר־יִלְכְּדֶנּוּ יְהֹוָה “the tribe which Jehovah will choose,” will mark out by lot; verse 17 Jos 7:17.

Niphal, pass. of Kal No. 1, Psa 9:16 Jer 51:56 2Ki 16:18 No. 3, 1Sa 10:20, 21 1Sa 10:21.

Hithpael, to take hold of one another, to adhere together (Arab. لكد Conj. V. to be joined together with the parts compacted), Job 41:9, יִתְלַכְּדוּ “they stick together (the scales of the crocodile);” Job 38:30, פְּנֵי תְהֹום יִתְלַכָּדוּ “the face of the waters adheres together,” is frozen. Compare אָחַז No. 4.

Derivatives, מַלְכֹּדֶת and-
